title: Colymbada nicolai
intro: Colymbada nicolai, also known as Nicolai's hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it offers several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Colymbada nicolai include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Nicolai's hawk's beard is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.

It is a close relative of the edible dandelion, and its classification can be broken down into more specific categories. At the rank of family, Colymbada nicolai belongs to Asteraceae, at the genus and species rank, it is classified as Colymbada nicolai.
